Answers: If a failure or a combination of failures occurs, the system becomes unserviceable. The output signal from the failed system adopts an arbitrary but safe state.

If a failure or a combination of failures occurs the system remains operative and generates an output signal within acceptable limits.

If a failure or a combination of failures occurs, the system becomes unserviceable. The output signal adopts a well defined failure state. Therefore the system is considered to be passive.
If a failure or a combination of failures occurs the system remains operative and generates an output signal within acceptable limits.

Questions.QuestionString: Fail-Operational mean
Answers: If a failure or a combination of failures occurs, the system becomes unserviceable and The output signal from the failed system adopts an arbitrary but safe state.

Questions.QuestionString: What is function of AP?

The autopilot controls the aircrafts flight automatically Pitch channel
The autopilot controls the aircrafts flight automatically Roll channel

Questions.QuestionString: In the inter loop, what is a result of comparison between actual attitude and commanded attitude?
In the outer loop, what is a result of comparison between actual flight path and desired path?
What is the function of the Yaw damper?
Answers: Control the rudder to compensate for changes in the center of gravity
Control the rudder to dampen dutch roll oscillations during the entire flight.
Control the rudder deflections as much as possible

Questions.QuestionString: When the autopilot disengages
Answers: There must be a warning which consists of a red flashing warning light or a corresponding message on the primary flight display and aural warning
There must be a warning which consists of a amber flashing warning light or a corresponding message on the primary flight display
Only appear corresponding message on the primary flight display and aural warning

Questions.QuestionString: What is the function of force transducer of Control Wheel Steering (CWS)
Answers: Convert a force to a proportional voltage generates for the autopilot
Indication value of force for pilot when using control column
Keep control column not moving manually with normal force

Questions.QuestionString: How does the autopilot work if there is a signal difference between two autopilot channels happens
which mode allows the pilot to select the desired climb or sink rate in feet per minute.
The autopilot rolls the aircraft to wings level and keeps the actual heading.
Answers: If the roll angle is less than five degrees
If the roll angle is less than ten degrees and pitch angle is less than five degrees
If the roll angle is less than ten degrees and pitch angle is less than ten degrees

Questions.QuestionString: How does the autopilot work when the aircraft reaches the cruise altitude
Answers: The autopilot works in common mode
The autopilot pitch channel works in altitude hold and speed mode
The autopilot pitch channel works in altitude hold and the roll channel still controls the aircraft on the calculated track.

Questions.QuestionString: Maximum deflection of the yaw damper input
in the speed trim subsystem, Speed stability means that
Answers: With increasing airspeed the aircraft must be trimmed in the nose up direction and with decreasing airspeed in nose down direction.
With decreasing airspeed the aircraft must be trimmed in the nose up direction and with increasing airspeed in nose down direction.
Threshold of actual airspeed is not overspeed.

Questions.QuestionString: When a failure in a trim system is detected
Answers: the second system takes over automatically
the second system is activated by the pilots.
the pilots must be trim manually

Questions.QuestionString: What is the classic autothrottle system
Answers: It uses a servomotor to adjust the throttle position and the mechanical linkage to the engines.
It delivers a digital signal directly to the FADEC computer of the engine.
It uses a servomotor to adjust the throttle position and control by FADEC computer

Questions.QuestionString: Which type of aircraft use the classic autothrottle system
What is the Thrust Mode of autothrottle system
Answers: It controls either the Engine Pressure Ratio, EPR or the N1 rotational speed to the desired value
It controls the calibrated airspeed of the aircraft. The desired value comes either from the FMS or can be selected by the pilot.
It controls the thrust by selected N2 rotational speed from the FMS

Questions.QuestionString: What is the Speed Mode of autothrottle system
Answers: It controls either the Engine Pressure Ratio, EPR or the N1 rotational speed to the desired value
It controls the calibrated airspeed of the aircraft. The desired value comes either from the FMS or can be selected by the pilot.
It controls the thrust by selected N2 rotational speed from the FMS

Questions.QuestionString: Which mode of the autothrottle system is used during Take off?
Answers: Either thrust or Speed mode is used depends on aircraft speed

Questions.QuestionString: Which mode of the autothrottle system is used during Climb?
Answers: Either thrust or Speed mode is used depends on aircraft speed
Either thrust or Speed mode is used depends primarily on the active autopilot or flight director pitch mode

Questions.QuestionString: Which mode of the autothrottle system is used during Cruise?
Answers: Either thrust or Speed mode is used depends on aircraft speed

Questions.QuestionString: During the approach phase the autothrottle system controls speed which depends on
Answers: The flap and landing gear positions

Questions.QuestionString: How long maximum thrust can be used when you select the TAKE OFF mode
Answers: This highest thrust limit must only be used for up to 5 minutes, because it produces high stress for the engines and reduces the engine life time
This highest thrust limit must only be used for up to 10 minutes, because it produces high stress for the engines and reduces the engine life time
This highest thrust limit must only be used for up to nose landing gear not compress, because it produces high stress for the engines and reduces the engine life time

Questions.QuestionString: Under what conditions does The thrust selects FLEXIBLE TAKE OFF mode ?
Answers: Maximum takeoff weights or long runways.
Low takeoff weights or long runways.

Questions.QuestionString: How to disconnect the autothrottle
Answers: When you move the switch on the panel to off or when any condition for autothrottle operation becomes invalid.
When you move the switch on the panel to off or push either autothrottle quick disconnect pushbutton.
When you move the switch on the panel to off or push either autothrottle quick disconnect pushbutton or when any condition for autothrottle operation becomes invalid.

Questions.QuestionString: During flare maneuver the autothrottle.
Answers: Retards throttles slowly to the idle position

Questions.QuestionString: The alpha trim subsystem is primarily used to
Answers: decrease the induced drag during low speed flights.
decrease the induced drag during high speed flights.
increase the induced drag during high speed flights.

Questions.QuestionString: Take Off Decision Speed (V1) mean:
Answers: is speed which the pilot has to be discontinued take off if V1 is not yet reached and a engine failure occurred or a warning/caution is generated by the warning system
is speed which the pilot has to be discontinued take off if V1 is reached with both engines working normal.
is speed which is the minimum speed to be obtained with one engine inoperative at a height of 35 ft above the runway surface.

Questions.QuestionString: Rotation Speed (VR) mean:
Answers: is speed which the pilot rotates the aircraft if V1 is not yet reached.
is the speed at which the pilot rotates the aircraft to increase lift for lift off.
is speed which is the minimum speed to be obtained with one engine inoperative at a height of 35 ft above the runway surface.

Questions.QuestionString: Take Off Safety Speed (V2) mean:
Answers: is speed which the pilot has to be discontinued take off if V2 is not yet reached with one engine inoperative
is the speed at which the pilot rotates the aircraft to increase lift for lift off.
is speed which is the minimum speed to be obtained with one engine inoperative at a height of 35 ft above the runway surface.

Questions.QuestionString: When a FD warning flag is displayed in the ADI this means that?
Answers: Flight Director computer has no electrical power available and/or the computer has a FAULT.

Questions.QuestionString: How many altitude mode are there in the pitch modes of auto flight systems?
Answers: 2 mode: the altitude select mode and the altitude hold mode

Questions.QuestionString: In AP/FD mode, the common mode consist of:
Answers: Approach mode, autoland mode, Take-off mode
Autoland mode, Take-off mode, Go-around mode
Approach mode, autoland mode, Take-off mode, Go-around mode

Questions.QuestionString: How many parts are there in the yaw damper system:
Answers: 3 parts: a yaw damper computer, a yaw damper servo motor, a rate gyro to detect the changes in yaw direction
2 parts: a yaw damper computer, a yaw damper servo motor
2 parts: a yaw damper computer, a rate gyro to detect the changes in yaw direction

Questions.QuestionString: What is the function of speed trim sub-system?
Answers: provides speed stability during high speed and high thrust operation during take off and go around
provides speed stability during low speed and high thrust operation during landing
provides speed stability during low speed and high thrust operation during take off and go around

Questions.QuestionString: What is the function of alpha trim sub-system?
Answers: to decrease the induced drag during high speed flights.
to increase the induced drag during high speed flights.
to decrease the induced drag during low speed flights.

Questions.QuestionString: What is the function of MACH trim sub-system?
Answers: It moves the elevator down when the MACH number increases
It moves the stabilizer leading edge down when the MACH number decrease
It moves the stabilizer leading edge down when the MACH number increases

Questions.QuestionString: in autopilot pitch trim, When a certain level of elevator deflection is exceeded for a few seconds, what's happen?
Answers: the trim computer starts the actuator of elevator
the trim computer starts the stabilizer trim motor.
the trim computer starts the actuator of aileron

Questions.QuestionString: What does data supply to speed adapter of autopilot system?
How to activates the link between the autopilot servomotor and the flight controls
in Autopilot, Where the actual Attitude data come from?
Answers: a vertical gyro or the IRU

Questions.QuestionString: What is the most important part of the inner loop?
Answers: the comparison between the actual attitude of the aircraft and the commanded attitude

Questions.QuestionString: Which statement is correct?
Answers: At lower airspeeds the control surface needs less deflection for a certain change of attitude
At higher airspeeds the control surface needs more deflection for a certain change of attitude
At higher airspeeds the control surface needs less deflection for a certain change of attitude

Questions.QuestionString: How to disconnect the autopilot?
Answers: You can disengage the autopilot by pressing the illuminated autopilot push button a second time or by pressing the red pushbutton on the side stick
You can disengage the autopilot by pressing the illuminated autopilot push button a second time
You can disengage the autopilot by pressing the red pushbutton on the side stick

Questions.QuestionString: What is the function of Servo or transfer valves are used in the power unit to deflect the surface.
Answers: convert the electrical signals from the control valve lever into a proportional hydraulic signal
convert the electrical signals from the autopilot into a proportional pneumatic signal
convert the electrical signals from the autopilot into a proportional hydraulic signal

Questions.QuestionString: What is the Head Up Display (HUD)?
Answers: A projector sends the important flight information to the glass mirror which is installed in the pilots line of sight during flight.
A projector sends the important flight information to the glass mirror which is installed in center line of cockpit windshield.
Reflects all information from the PFDs onto the Captain and First office windshields

Questions.QuestionString: Why does the pilot can move the throttle easily to the new position If applies force to a throttle.
Answers: Due to the pilot applies force more than brake force of brake dum.
Due to a small actuator is turned and lifts the brake shoes from the brake drum so that the brake opens
Due to the throttle only keep by a small spring

Questions.QuestionString: Decision height (DH) is
Answers: The height above the runway threshold
The height above the ground (radio height)
The height above the sea level

Questions.QuestionString: Alert Height (AH) is
Answers: The height above the runway threshold
The height above the ground (radio height)
The height above the sea level

Questions.QuestionString: When is autothrottle system automatically disengaged?.
In an autopilot, what controls pitch mode?

Autopilot, Flight Director, utothrottle System and Yaw Damper System
Autopilot, Flight Director, utothrottle System, Automatic Pitch Trim System and Yaw Damper System

Questions.QuestionString: What is the function of Flight envelope protection
Answers: Monitor the operation of Autoflight systems to prevent any abnormal flight condition during automatic flight
Maintain aircraft stability during all phases of the flight
Keep Aircraft not overspeed during the whole flight

Questions.QuestionString: When do both autopilots work together

By pressing the red pushbutton on the side stick
By pressing the illuminated autopilot push button a second time or by pressing the red pushbutton on the side stick

Questions.QuestionString: How many loops are there in the Autopilot system

By roll bar and pitch bar indicator either in the Attitude Director Indicator (ADI) or in the modern replacement called the Primary Flight Display (PFD)
By yaw bar and pitch bar indicator either in the Attitude Director Indicator (ADI) or in the modern replacement called the Primary Flight Display (PFD)

Questions.QuestionString: In the auto flight system Navigation modes are used to
Answers: Control the track of the aircraft.
Control the altitude of the aircraft.

Questions.QuestionString: The rudder can be deflected by the pilot via
Answers: The rudder pedals or the rudder trim function.
